title: सरस्वती गायत्री मन्त्र
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सरस्वती गायत्री मन्त्रः ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
या कुन्देन्दुतुषारहारधवला या शुभ्रवस्त्रावृता
या वीणावरदण्डमण्डितकरा या श्वेतपद्मासना।
या ब्रह्माच्युतशङ्करप्रभृतिभिर्देवैः सदा पूजिता
सा मां पातु सरस्वती भगवती निःशेषजाड्यापहा॥

शुक्लां ब्रह्मविचारसारपरमामाद्यां जगद्व्यापिनीं
वीणापुस्तकधारिणीमभयदां जाड्यान्धकारापहाम्।
हस्ते स्फाटिकमालिकां विदधतीं पद्मासने संस्थिताम्
वन्दे तां परमेश्वरीं भगवतीं बुद्धिप्रदां शारदाम्॥

॥ सरस्वती गायत्री ॥
ॐ वाग्देव्यै च विद्महे
ब्रह्मपत्न्यै च धीमहि।
तन्नो वाणी प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ अपर सरस्वती गायत्री ॥
ॐ सरस्वत्यै विद्महे
ब्रह्मपुत्र्यै धीमहि।
तन्नो देवी प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
सरस्वतीगायत्रीमेतां यः पठेत् सततं नरः।
विद्यां बुद्धिं च मेधां च वाक्सिद्धिं लभते ध्रुवम्॥

॥ इति श्री सरस्वती गायत्री मन्त्रः सम्पूर्णः ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Saraswati Gayatri Mantrah ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Ya Kundendu-tushara-hara-dhavala Ya Shubhra-vastra-avrita
Ya Veena-vara-danda-mandita-kara Ya Shveta-padmasana.
Ya Brahma-achyuta-shankara-prabhritibhir-Devaih Sada Poojita
Sa Mam Patu Saraswati Bhagavati Nihshesha-jadyapaha.

Shuklam Brahmavichara-sara-paramam-adyam Jagadvyapineem
Veena-pustaka-dharinim-abhaya-dam Jadyandhakarapahaam.
Haste Sphatika-malikam Vidadhateem Padmasane Samsthitam
Vande Tam Parameshvarim Bhagavatim Buddhipradam Sharadam.

|| Saraswati Gayatri ||
Om Vagdevyai Cha Vidmahe
Brahma-patnyai Cha Dheemahi.
Tanno Vani Prachodayat.

|| Apara Saraswati Gayatri ||
Om Sarasvatyai Vidmahe
Brahma-putryai Dheemahi.
Tanno Devi Prachodayat.

|| Phalashruti ||
Saraswati-gayatrim-etam Yah Pathet Satatam Narah.
Vidyam Buddhim Cha Medham Cha Vak-siddhim Labhate Dhruvam.

|| Iti Shri Saraswati Gayatri Mantrah Sampurnah ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान: जो कुन्द, चन्द्र, तुषार, हार के समान धवल हैं, शुभ्र वस्त्र से आवृत हैं, वीणा-दण्ड से मण्डित हाथ वाली हैं, श्वेत पद्मासन पर विराजमान हैं, ब्रह्मा-विष्णु-शंकर आदि देवों द्वारा सदा पूजित हैं, वे निःशेष जड़ता का अपहरण करने वाली सरस्वती भगवती मेरी रक्षा करें।

शुक्लवर्णा, ब्रह्मविचार-सारभूत, आद्या, जगद्व्यापिनी, वीणा-पुस्तक धारिणी, अभयदायिनी, जड़तान्धकार नाशिनी, स्फटिकमाला धारिणी, पद्मासनस्थिता, बुद्धिप्रदा शारदा परमेश्वरी को वन्दन।

सरस्वती गायत्री: हम वाग्देवी को जानते हैं, ब्रह्मपत्नी का ध्यान करते हैं। वे वाणी हमारी बुद्धि को प्रेरित करें।

जो इस सरस्वती गायत्री का सतत पाठ करता है, उसे विद्या, बुद्धि, मेधा और वाक्सिद्धि निश्चित प्राप्त होती है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीसरस्वतीगायत्रीमन्त्रस्य ब्रह्मा ऋषिः। गायत्री छन्दः। श्री सरस्वती (वाग्देवी) देवता। ॐ ऐं बीजम्। सरस्वती शक्तिः। विद्या-बुद्धि-मेधा प्राप्ति कीलकम्। श्रीसरस्वतीप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Invokes the blessing of vak siddhi — mastery over speech and expression
Contains the famous Ya Kundendu dhyanam shloka
Ideal for Vasant Panchami, Thursdays, and exam seasons
Traditionally recited for removal of jadya (dullness) and bestows clarity of thought
Two variations covering Vagdevi and Brahmaputri aspects
description: The Saraswati Gayatri Mantra, known in Sanskrit as सरस्वती गायत्री मन्त्र, is a powerful Vedic meditation mantra in praise of Goddess Saraswati. This sacred composition is sourced from Saraswati Rahasya Upanishad and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to remove Jadya (dullness) and bestow clarity of thought as well as contain the famous Ya Kundendu dhyanam shloka. Additionally, it is known to bestow Vak Siddhi — mastery over speech and expression. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and during Vasant Panchami and Navratri. With 4 verses and a reading time of about 4 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Saraswati Gayatri Mantra in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Saraswati Gayatri Mantra with dhyanam in Sanskrit and Hindi meaning. Invoke Goddess Saraswati for knowledge, wisdom, and Vak Siddhi.
alsoKnownAs: Ya Kundendu Tushara Hara Dhavala Saraswati Stotra

seoFaqs.question: What is the Saraswati Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Saraswati Gayatri Mantra (सरस्वती गायत्री मन्त्र) is a Vedic-format Gayatri prayer dedicated to Goddess Saraswati — the goddess of vidya (knowledge), buddhi (intellect), medha (memory), vak (speech) and the arts (kala) — preserved in the Saraswati Rahasya Upanishad and the broader Devi-Upanishadic tradition. The composition has four parts: (a) the famous Ya Kundendu Tushara Hara Dhavala dhyana shloka picturing Saraswati as white as jasmine, moon, snow and pearl-strand, holding the veena and seated on the white lotus; (b) the Shuklam Brahmavichara Sara dhyana shloka emphasizing her role as the essence of Brahman-inquiry; (c) the principal Saraswati Gayatri (Om Vagdevyai cha vidmahe Brahmapatnyai cha dhimahi tanno Vani prachodayat); (d) an alternate Gayatri (Om Sarasvatyai vidmahe Brahmaputryai dhimahi tanno Devi prachodayat). (Source: Saraswati Rahasya Upanishad.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the Saraswati Gayatri mean word by word?
seoFaqs.answer: The principal Saraswati Gayatri reads: Om Vagdevyai cha Vidmahe Brahmapatnyai cha Dhimahi Tanno Vani Prachodayat. Om — the pranava; Vagdevyai cha — 'and to the Goddess of Speech (Vagdevi)'; vidmahe — 'may we know'; Brahmapatnyai cha — 'and to the consort of Brahma'; dhimahi — 'may we meditate'; tannah — 'unto us'; Vani — 'may that Goddess of Speech (Vani)'; prachodayat — 'inspire / impel.' Together: 'Om, may we know the Goddess of Speech, may we meditate on the consort of Brahma; may that Vani inspire us.' The mantra carries the classical vidmahe-dhimahi-prachodayat triplet of all Gayatri-format mantras and specifically requests Saraswati's inspiration of speech and intellect. (Source: Saraswati Gayatri text.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous 'Ya Kundendu' dhyana shloka in the Saraswati Gayatri?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ya Kundendu Tushara Hara Dhavala dhyana opens the Saraswati Gayatri text and is one of the most beloved Devi-dhyana shlokas in all Sanskrit literature. Translation: 'She who is white as the jasmine flower, the moon, freshly fallen snow, and the strand of pearls; she who is robed in pure white garments; she whose hands are adorned with the noble veena; she who is seated upon the white lotus; she who is ever worshipped by Brahma, Achyuta (Vishnu), Shankara (Shiva) and the other gods — may that Bhagavati Saraswati protect me, removing all jadya (dullness) without remainder.' The verse is recited at the start of every Saraswati puja, every academic activity, every musical performance, and at the opening of every learning session in traditional gurukulas. (Source: Saraswati Gayatri Ya Kundendu dhyana shloka.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Saraswati Gayatri be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Saraswati Gayatri is most powerful on Thursday (Guruvar — sacred to Brihaspati and Saraswati) and is especially auspicious on Vasant Panchami (Magha Shukla Panchami — Saraswati's birth-festival), Sharad Navratri (especially the last three days when Saraswati is worshipped after Lakshmi and Durga), Vijayadashami / Dussehra (when academic instruments and books are worshipped in Ayudha Puja), Akshara Abhyasam (the child's first writing ceremony), Vidyarambham (commencement of learning), the day before examinations, the start of every academic year, and every morning before commencing study or teaching. The chanter sits facing east on a white asana, wears white, places the Saraswati image and books before them, lights white camphor, offers white flowers (lotus, jasmine), and chants 108 times with focus on the dhyana imagery. (Source: Saraswati puja paddhati; Vasant Panchami tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Saraswati Gayatri?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares: Saraswati-gayatrim-etam Yah Pathet Satatam Narah, Vidyam Buddhim Cha Medham Cha Vak-siddhim Labhate Dhruvam — 'one who continuously recites this Saraswati Gayatri certainly attains vidya (knowledge), buddhi (intellect), medha (memory) and vak-siddhi (mastery over speech).' Traditional benefits include: removal of jadya (dullness, mental sluggishness) as promised by the dhyana shloka; bestowal of academic success and exam preparation; enhanced memory and retention for students; clarity of speech for orators, teachers, lawyers and writers; mastery in music, dance and the fine arts (kala-siddhi); protection of children's education; awakening of poetic inspiration (kavi-vakra); ability to grasp difficult texts and teachings; and ultimately Brahma-vidya — knowledge of the Self. (Source: Saraswati Gayatri phalashruti; Saraswati Rahasya Upanishad.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Saraswati Gayatri compare with the Saraswati Stotra and Saraswati Stuti?
seoFaqs.answer: The Saraswati Gayatri is a compact mantra-format text (4-6 verses, ~4 minutes) ideal for daily japa and for opening any learning activity — its specific function is mantra-japa for Saraswati's inspiration. The various Saraswati Stotras (such as Saraswati Stotra by Sage Yajnavalkya) and Saraswati Stutis are longer praise-hymns describing Saraswati's many forms, leelas and gifts — recited for darshana, devotional contemplation and on major Saraswati festivals. The Saraswati Sahasranama and Saraswati Ashtottara Shatanamavali are namavali-format texts for archana worship. Devotees commonly recite the Gayatri daily, the Stotra for Saraswati festivals, the Ashtottara for archana puja, and all together on Vasant Panchami and Vijayadashami. (Source: Saraswati stotra-paddhati.)
